term: etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā
preferred_translation: this is mine, I am this, this is my self
alternative_translations: this is mine, this I am, this is my self
discouraged_translations: this belongs to me, this is who I really am, this is the self
definition: Canonical three-part selfing formula of ownership, identification, and self-claim.
notes: Use this record when a text explicitly spells out the movement into appropriation. Keep the three turns distinct: possession, I-am identification, and self-claim. MN 148 uses the formula as the live pattern of identity-arising rather than as an abstract doctrinal slogan.

example_phrases.pali: etaṁ mama, esohamasmi, eso me attā
example_phrases.translation: this is mine, I am this, this is my self
example_phrases.notes: Control line for the positive selfing pattern in MN 148.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the formula as the explicit path leading to the arising of identity.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the positive selfing formula.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not soften the line into generic identity language and do not collapse the three movements into one vague paraphrase.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents explicit appropriation language from drifting into milder ownership or self-esteem phrasing.
